methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Occam, a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith, has raised $14M to date. The valuation exercise is anchored within a peer universe of 3 public biotech & health companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three triangulation methods evaluated, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the $14M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage progression in biotech, yielding an implied valuation of $35M. Peer-Median analysis was not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow peer set (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage identified). Public-Comp Anchoring, while supported by a 6.6x P/S median across the three public tickers, could not be applied absent disclosed revenue for the target company.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $30M to $40M, with $35M as the central point. This range rests on a single triangulation method, creating meaningful uncertainty. Key risks to valuation include the relative youth of the capital base ($14M raised), the broad heterogeneity of private peers, and absence of revenue or clinical milestone visibility. Additional diligence on burn rate, runway, and clinical/product progress would be essential to narrow the range.
